name: Ecopetrol S.A.

profile.description: Ecopetrol S.A. is Colombia's largest oil and gas company, a state-controlled integrated energy producer operating across exploration, production, transportation, and refining.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Revenue declined 17.2% YoY reflecting lower oil prices and production challenges. Earnings fell 60.8% YoY [1]. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI products. Market Share (1/2): Dominant in Colombian oil production but a mid-tier global producer. Pipeline (0/2): Limited new project visibility. R&D Intensity (1/1): Energy transition R&D investment above industry average [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Energy production mission connected to Colombian national development. Community Impact (1/2): Significant social investment as state-controlled enterprise. Employee Retention (0/2): No data. Insider Buying (1/2): Government majority ownership provides alignment [1].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Ecopetrol shows mixed financials. Forward P/E of 11.04 is attractive, profit margin of 7.5% is below target, and ROE of 11.8% approaches target [1]. D/E of 99.96% is moderate for an integrated oil company. Revenue declined 17.2% YoY. Dividend yield of 4.43% with 95.6% payout is generous but stretched. Liquidity cleared at $51.2M daily [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Oil and gas production is physical-world infrastructure immune to AI displacement. Colombian operations involve complex geology, refining, and transportation that AI cannot substitute [1]. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: Ecopetrol plays a mixed game — long-term national energy security mission but short-term pressure from government dividend demands and oil price dependence.

just_cause.analysis: Shallow cause (1 pt → 4/10): Colombian energy security and development connects to national purpose [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Low trust (0 pts → 2/10): State-controlled enterprise with political interference risks [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Infinite (1 pt → 10/10): Acknowledges Petrobras and other national oil companies as benchmarks [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Some courage (1 pt → 6/10): Energy transition investments despite political pressure for dividends [1].

investment_thesis: Ecopetrol offers a 4.43% dividend yield with forward P/E of 11.04, providing value characteristics for a national oil company [1].
thesis_continued: Revenue declined 17.2% and earnings fell 60.8% YoY, reflecting oil price and production challenges. State control creates political risk for capital allocation and dividend sustainability at 95.6% payout [1].
key_catalysts: Oil price recovery supporting revenue and earnings [1]
Energy transition investments diversifying revenue streams [1]
key_risks: Colombian political risk and government interference in capital allocation [1]
Oil price dependence with 95.6% dividend payout leaving no buffer [1]
Revenue decline of 17.2% YoY with production challenges [1]
sources: Yahoo Finance / yfinance data for EC (Ecopetrol), retrieved April 2026
